sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 9.43 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.9)

statement.latex: b) The group$G$indicated in (N.\,D.\,Podufalov, Abstracts of the 9th All--Union Symp. on Group Theory, Moscow, 1984, 113--114 (Russian)) allows us to construct a projective plane of order 3: one can take as lines any class of subgroups of order$2 5 11 17$conjugate under$S$and add four more lines in a natural way. In a similar way, one can construct projective planes of order$p^n$for any prime$p$an...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
